summary: Joel announces that some of his monthly columns for the Programmer's Paradise catalog are now available online, including reviews of VMWare and ERwin. He explains his policy for writing product reviews: he only reviews tools he actually uses in his daily work at Fog Creek Software. He notes that unlike trade magazine reviewers, he still spends about half his time doing actual software development. The post also briefly mentions upcoming projects including integrating an image editor into CityDesk and signing a lease for new office space.
key_points: Joel maintains a strict review policy of only covering products he genuinely uses in his daily work, distinguishing himself from trade publication reviewers
He emphasizes that he still spends roughly 50% of his time doing hands-on software development despite running Fog Creek
The 750-word column format limits depth but forces focus on practical, real-world product experiences
Fog Creek is expanding into new office space with an architect-designed floorplan featuring parallelogram-shaped offices
money_quotes: I do have a policy for writing reviews: I only review products that I actually want to use in my daily work at Fog Creek.
Unlike trade magazine reviewers, I still spend about 50% of my time doing software development, and I'm happy to try the latest toys if I think they will help.
key_insight: Product reviews are most credible when written by practitioners who actually use the tools in real work, not by professional reviewers evaluating them in isolation.
